label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which statement is NOT an advantage of the algorithm described. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the details. The monitor watches deceleration and distance from a light, and guesses which cars might break the Highway Code. It gives a two-second warning if another car is going to cross a crossroads, which is enough time for others to brake. MIT's Professor Jonathan How says future smart cars might use this system to help drivers foresee and avoid accidents. They tested the algorithm in Virginia and found it accurately identified potential violators a couple of seconds before reaching a red light, giving enough time for other drivers to react if alerted. Compared to other models, this MIT algorithm has fewer false alarms, which is good because it's for human drivers. The findings are going to be published in IEEE Transactions. Now, the question is asking which statement is NOT an advantage. Let's look at the options: (A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. (B) It usually produces fewer false alarms. (C) It helps drivers avoid potential accidents by judging the threats. (D) The monitor of this algorithm can judge the traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. First, let's check each option against the
